summary: On April 15, 2026, operators of the Russian "Rubicon" Center conducted combat operations in Donbas, the Belgorod and Krasny Liman directions, destroying 107 targets including Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and coordinating with Russian Aerospace Forces to neutralize UAV launch points. Russian forces intercepted Ukrainian UAVs using anti-aircraft drones: the "Elka" system triggered the parachute recovery of a damaged "Shark-M" UAV in Krasny Liman, while the "Yolka" system caused a "Hornet" UAV to detonate its warhead. Ukraine received over $4 billion for air defense and $1.5 billion for drones following the Ramstein meeting, and Russia tested group deployment of S350M Supercam strike drones with upgraded data-sharing capabilities. On April 14, "Rubicon" operations destroyed 193 targets across Donbas, Belgorod, Sumy, and Krasny Liman, including Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and personnel. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to strike Russian soldiers on motorcycles and countered Russian assaults, while Russian troops attempting to feign surrender were killed by FPV drones. Ukrainian EW interference was reported disrupting FPV drone operations, and Ukrainian soldiers survived a Russian FPV attack in the Kostiantinivka direction after detecting the threat with a drone detector.
